TSLA: 'Largest chip manufacturing facility ever': Elon Musk to build part of Terafab in Austin. Elon Musk announced plans for Tesla and SpaceX to build the world's largest chip manufacturing facility, dubbed "Terafab," in Austin, Texas, to produce chips for AI and space data centers. While a small chip design facility will be located at Tesla's Gigafactory Texas, the main Terafab will be a massive 100 million square feet, dwarfing Samsung's plant in Taylor, with locations still under consideration. This ambitious project aims to meet the companies' growing need for semiconductors, which Musk states current suppliers cannot provide at the desired pace.

Norwegian, Freeport-McMoRan, Super Micro, Tesla, United, Synopsys, and More Stock Movers. Stock futures were rising Monday, a trend that continued into the trading day for several companies including Norwegian, Super Micro, Freeport-McMoRan, Tesla, United Airlines, and Synopsys. This positive market movement was attributed to hopes for a de-escalation of tensions in the Middle East following comments from former President Trump. The article suggests that "productive conversations" between the U.S. and Iran have boosted investor confidence.

Elon Musk Unveils Ambitious Terafab Plans For Tesla, SpaceX: 'We're Starting A Galactic Civilization'. Elon Musk has announced the Terafab project, a joint venture between Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI, aimed at revolutionizing chip manufacturing for both space and AI applications. This ambitious undertaking seeks to produce over a terawatt of compute per year, with a significant portion dedicated to space. Musk envisions the Terafab as a comprehensive facility that will integrate all stages of chip development, supporting autonomous vehicles, humanoid robots, and solar-powered AI satellites, with an eye towards establishing an industrial base on the moon and a "galactic civilization."

TGT: AT&T Inc. $T Shares Acquired by Avanza Fonder AB. Avanza Fonder AB increased its stake in AT&T Inc. by 10.6% in the fourth quarter, acquiring 56,009 additional shares, bringing their total holdings to 584,097 shares valued at $14.51 million. AT&T reported strong Q4 earnings, surpassing expectations with $0.52 EPS and $33.47 billion in revenue, and set an optimistic FY2026 EPS guidance. Wall Street analysts maintain a bullish outlook, with several firms raising price targets, further supported by AT&T's plans to increase prices for legacy unlimited plans, which could enhance revenue and margins.

Target Corp. stock underperforms Monday when compared to competitors despite daily gains. Target Corp. (TGT) shares rose 1.47% on Monday to $114.93, breaking a five-day losing streak, despite the company's stock underperforming compared to its competitors. This occurred during a favorable trading session for the broader market, with the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average both seeing gains.
SBUX: Starbucks Corp. stock faces pressure after Q1 FY2026 earnings miss amid cost-cut push. Starbucks Corp. reported Q1 FY2026 earnings below expectations, causing its stock to drop 1.92% despite revenue topping forecasts. CEO Brian Niccol introduced a "Back to Starbucks" strategy focused on hospitality and a $2 billion cost-saving initiative to address market pressures, rising competition, and increased costs. US investors are closely watching the company's turnaround potential, with analysts maintaining a "Moderate Buy" rating.

Starbucks Corp. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day. Starbucks Corp. (SBUX) stock increased by 1.38% to $93.83 on Monday, outperforming its competitors and the broader market. This strong performance occurred on a favorable trading day where both the S&P 500 Index and the Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw significant gains. Despite this rise, Starbucks' stock remains 10.48% below its 52-week high.

Taco Bell, Dunkin’ franchisee to pay $1.5 mln in NYC scheduling case. A Taco Bell and Dunkin' franchisee, Salz Management LLC, has agreed to pay over $1.5 million to settle claims by New York City that it violated local laws regarding worker scheduling practices. The city alleged the franchisee failed to provide advance notice of schedules, pay for "clopening" shifts, and offer existing workers available shifts before hiring new staff across two dozen restaurants. This settlement follows a larger $38.9 million settlement with Starbucks in December for similar violations, and the city also announced a new lawsuit against another Dunkin' franchisee, QSR Management LLC, for alleged scheduling law breaches affecting about 1,000 workers.
DIS: The Walt Disney Company $DIS Shares Sold by E. Ohman J or Asset Management AB. E. Ohman J or Asset Management AB significantly reduced its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 16.6% in Q4 2025, selling over 12,000 shares. Despite this, major institutional investors like Norges Bank and Viking Global made substantial new investments, indicating strong overall institutional confidence with 65.71% ownership. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $134.13, following Disney's Q4 earnings beat.
